description: Complete GPIO pinout for Raspberry Pi 3B+ with PiCar Pro module assignments
description_ru: Полная распиновка GPIO Raspberry Pi 3B+ с привязкой к модулям PiCar Pro

pins.name: 3.3V Power
pins.notes: 3.3V power supply rail — used by I2C devices (PCA9685, ADS7830, SSD1306, MPU6050)

pins.name: 5V Power
pins.notes: 5V power supply rail — used by HC-SR04, WS2812B LED power, L298N logic

pins.name: I2C1 Data
pins.notes: I2C bus 1 data line — shared by all I2C devices (PCA9685, ADS7830, SSD1306, MPU6050)

pins.name: 5V Power
pins.notes: 5V power supply rail — secondary 5V pin

pins.name: I2C1 Clock
pins.notes: I2C bus 1 clock line — shared by all I2C devices (PCA9685, ADS7830, SSD1306, MPU6050)

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: Motor A Enable
pins.notes: EN_A — PWM speed control for left motor (Motor A)

pins.name: UART Transmit
pins.notes: UART0 transmit — available for serial communication (unused by PiCar Pro)

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: UART Receive
pins.notes: UART0 receive — available for serial communication (unused by PiCar Pro)

pins.name: Motor B Enable
pins.notes: EN_B — PWM speed control for right motor (Motor B)

pins.name: Motor B IN2 / Buzzer
pins.notes: CONFLICT: Used as IN2_B for motor direction AND buzzer output. GPIO 18 supports hardware PWM0 — suitable for buzzer tone generation. Verify configuration to avoid conflict.

pins.name: Motor B IN1
pins.notes: IN1_B — Motor B direction control input 1 (right motor)

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: General Purpose
pins.notes: Available GPIO — unused by PiCar Pro

pins.name: General Purpose
pins.notes: Available GPIO — unused by PiCar Pro

pins.name: 3.3V Power
pins.notes: 3.3V power supply rail — secondary 3.3V pin

pins.name: General Purpose
pins.notes: Available GPIO — unused by PiCar Pro

pins.name: SPI0 MOSI
pins.notes: SPI0 Master Out Slave In — used to drive WS2812B addressable LEDs via SPI data

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: SPI0 MISO
pins.notes: SPI0 Master In Slave Out — unused by PiCar Pro (WS2812B is write-only)

pins.name: General Purpose
pins.notes: Available GPIO — unused by PiCar Pro

pins.name: SPI0 Clock / Ultrasonic Trigger
pins.notes: CONFLICT: SPI0 clock is needed for WS2812B via SPI, but GPIO 11 is also used as HC-SR04 TRIG. If both SPI and ultrasonic are used simultaneously, reassign ultrasonic trigger to another GPIO. The SPI SCLK is essential for WS2812B data timing.

pins.name: SPI0 Chip Enable 0 / Ultrasonic Echo
pins.notes: CONFLICT: SPI0 CE0 is used for HC-SR04 ECHO input. If WS2812B uses SPI0, the chip select may need reconfiguration. Note: ECHO is 5V — requires voltage divider for 3.3V GPIO safety.

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: SPI0 Chip Enable 1
pins.notes: SPI0 chip enable 1 — available for additional SPI device (unused by PiCar Pro)

pins.name: I2C0 Data (HAT ID EEPROM)
pins.notes: I2C0 data — reserved for HAT ID EEPROM detection (not for general use)

pins.name: I2C0 Clock (HAT ID EEPROM)
pins.notes: I2C0 clock — reserved for HAT ID EEPROM detection (not for general use)

pins.name: Switch 1
pins.notes: Switch input 1 — digital read with internal pull-up enabled

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: Switch 2
pins.notes: Switch input 2 — digital read with internal pull-up enabled

pins.name: General Purpose (PWM0)
pins.notes: Available GPIO — supports hardware PWM0. Unused by PiCar Pro.

pins.name: Switch 3
pins.notes: Switch input 3 — digital read with internal pull-up enabled

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: Line Tracker Right
pins.notes: Line tracker right sensor — digital input from IR reflectance sensor

pins.name: Line Tracker Center
pins.notes: Line tracker center sensor — digital input from IR reflectance sensor

pins.name: Motor A IN1
pins.notes: IN1_A — Motor A direction control input 1 (left motor)

pins.name: Line Tracker Left
pins.notes: Line tracker left sensor — digital input from IR reflectance sensor

pins.name: Ground
pins.notes: Common ground

pins.name: Motor A IN2
pins.notes: IN2_A — Motor A direction control input 2 (left motor)

pin_conflicts.conflict: GPIO 18 is assigned to both L298N IN2_B (motor direction) and Buzzer. Only one function can be active at a time.
pin_conflicts.resolution: Verify in config.py which function takes priority. Consider moving buzzer to an unused GPIO if simultaneous use is needed.

pin_conflicts.conflict: GPIO 11 is the SPI0 SCLK pin, needed for WS2812B SPI communication. It is also used as HC-SR04 TRIG.
pin_conflicts.resolution: If WS2812B is driven via SPI0, HC-SR04 TRIG must be moved to another GPIO. Alternatively, bit-bang WS2812B data on a different GPIO.

pin_conflicts.conflict: GPIO 8 is the SPI0 CE0 pin, part of the SPI0 bus. It is also used as HC-SR04 ECHO input.
pin_conflicts.resolution: If SPI0 is fully used for WS2812B, HC-SR04 ECHO must be moved to another GPIO. WS2812B SPI mode may not require CE0, but bus contention should be verified.

devices.name: WS2812B LED Strip (via SPI MOSI)
devices.notes: Uses MOSI only. SCLK required for SPI clock. CE0/CE1 not needed for data.
